text.title: ACME TLS Renewal Automation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the cicd-tls-renewal-automation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: ACME renewal MUST run under a systemd timer with `OnFailure=` alert hook; cron is forbidden because failed renewals are silent.
rule.rationale: Cron mail-only failure reporting is ignored at scale; systemd integrates with journald + alerting.

rule.statement: A renewal hook MUST gracefully reload the consuming service (`nginx -s reload`, `systemctl reload haproxy`); without it, a renewed cert sits unused until next deploy.
rule.rationale: Cert file refreshed on disk doesn't reach the listener without reload; the practical effect is no renewal.

rule.statement: First-time ACME issuance MUST run against the issuer's staging environment first, then production; rate-limit lockouts from a misconfigured prod attempt take 7 days to recover.
rule.rationale: ACME rate limits are unforgiving; the staging endpoint exists specifically to avoid them.

rule.statement: Prometheus / blackbox_exporter MUST alert when any served cert has < 14 days to expiry, independent of the renewal job's own logs.
rule.rationale: Renewal job success ≠ cert delivered; only a probe against the live listener confirms the chain.

rule.statement: Wildcard certs (`*.example.com`) MUST use DNS-01 challenge; HTTP-01 MUST NOT be attempted for wildcards.
rule.rationale: HTTP-01 cannot satisfy a wildcard; the issuer will reject and rate-limit budget burns.

rule.statement: If TLS is managed by ACM / GCP managed cert / Cloudflare proxy with auto-renewal, this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Self-hosted ACME duplicates the managed path and creates two rotation surfaces.
